app.name: Hopp
app.one_liner: An open-source pair programming app enabling real-time collaboration with high-quality screen sharing via WebRTC.

screens.name: Main Collaboration Screen
screens.purpose: Host real-time pair programming sessions with screen sharing and participant controls.

screens.name: Settings Screen
screens.purpose: Configure user preferences and application behavior.

rust_commands.name: join_room
rust_commands.purpose: Initiate connection to a LiveKit-powered collaboration room from the Rust backend.

rust_commands.name: start_screen_share
rust_commands.purpose: Begin optimized screen sharing stream using WebRTC logic migrated to Rust.

mvp_plan: week 1: Implement main screen UI with React/TypeScript
week 1: Integrate LiveKit client for WebRTC signaling
week 1: Set up Tauri + Rust core with basic room joining
acceptance_tests: Settings page loads and allows saving of user preferences.
User can join a room and see their own screen shared with <500ms latency.
